title: Cloud-native AI layer for EuroHPC AI Factories
reason: AI Factories create compute capacity, but HPC systems are not naturally cloud-native. A layer that makes EuroHPC resources usable like cloud infrastructure could unlock startups/SMEs.

title: European GPU allocation and observability broker
reason: Compute is a critical red dependency. A broker/observability layer could help startups discover, reserve, route and optimize scarce GPU/HPC capacity across European providers.

title: AI Act evidence and compliance automation for regulated AI
reason: EU AI deployment will require evidence, governance, risk documentation and compliance workflows. This is accessible to a software startup and strongly relevant to France/EU.

title: Secure sovereign RAG/document AI for public-sector workflows
reason: European public institutions and regulated companies need private GenAI over sensitive documents, with sovereignty and traceability. French positioning is strong through LightOn, Orange, AI2F and public programs.

title: Model supply-chain security scanner for AI hubs and enterprises
reason: Model registries and open model reuse create supply-chain risk. A scanner that checks licenses, provenance, unsafe serialization and known risk patterns could be useful for enterprises and public AI deployments.

title: EU inference cost optimizer and routing layer
reason: Inference cost and latency will become a bottleneck for enterprises deploying AI. A vLLM/TGI/Kubernetes-based optimizer could run across European GPU/cloud/HPC providers.

title: AI data center energy/cooling intelligence for Europe
reason: AI Gigafactories and AI clouds depend on power capacity, cooling and energy efficiency. Software monitoring/optimization around power and cooling has B2B value and sovereignty relevance.

title: European language/domain data factory
reason: Europe has many languages and domain-specific public/industrial data. A secure data curation/evaluation pipeline for EU languages and sectors can serve specialized model builders.

title: Startup access layer for AI Factories
reason: AI Factories offer compute and support, but startups need discovery, application assistance, usage tracking and workload packaging. Build the front door for the compute network.

title: Sovereign AI dependency dashboard for VCs/corporates/public agencies
reason: The graph itself can become a B2B product: dependency visibility, opportunity extraction and strategic positioning for investors, industrial groups and policy actors.

title: Defense-adjacent secure AI deployment stack
reason: Companies like Helsing show defense AI is software-native. A civilian-compliant secure AI deployment layer can later cross into defense-adjacent regulated environments.

title: AI chip dependency intelligence for European procurement
reason: European AI builders are exposed to US accelerators and global foundry bottlenecks. A procurement/dependency intelligence tool can help buyers plan alternatives and risk exposure.
